title: Bind canonical route evidence to reviewed shadow outcomes
goal: Implement the minimal proposal-only shadow capture required by the Vibe-Lab routing-readiness experiment by reusing Grabowski route_evidence and binding it to independently reviewed semantic outcomes with primary evidence references.

acceptance.assertion: Shadow capture consumes existing validated hash-bound Grabowski route_evidence and does not infer canonical routing truth from argv or create a second route identity.

A minimal versioned JSON Schema defines the shadow record, including stable eligible-case identity, canonical route-evidence reference, bounded pre-outcome features, semantic outcome or explicit abstention, primary evidence references, capture timestamps and a no-effect boundary; schema validation fails closed.
Captured outcome separates process lifecycle from independently reviewed semantic task correctness or decision quality and supports abstention when no semantic outcome is available.
Every non-abstaining semantic outcome is bound to primary evidence references such as CI, diff-bound review, explicit operator decision or bounded Chronik evidence.
Capture has no automatic routing, policy, queue, merge or runtime authority and cannot alter the route of the observed task.
Learning records exclude raw prompts, transcripts, private notes and unrestricted argv payloads; only bounded task-class, risk, context and evidence identities are allowed.
Output can populate Vibe-Lab experiment 2026-07-23_operator-routing-ml-readiness-shadow without granting Vibe-Lab execution authority.
Focused regression tests cover missing route evidence, abstaining outcomes, evidence binding, redaction and non-effect behavior.

metadata.source: User request 2026-07-23 plus live Grabowski/Vibe-Lab readiness audit
metadata.reason: The live task table has substantial execution history but no direct canonical route or semantic-outcome columns. Verified route_evidence exists separately in Agent Workspace manifests, but the current sources expose no explicit task-store join binding to independently reviewed semantic outcomes; process state is not a valid supervised-routing quality label.

validated_acceptance.reuse-canonical-route-evidence: Merged Grabowski PR #410 reuses Agent Workspace route_evidence through grabowski_agent_workspace._normalize_route_evidence(), requires verified and evidence_complete route evidence, deterministically replays persisted normalized evidence, and never derives canonical route truth from argv.
validated_acceptance.versioned-capture-schema: Merged contracts/operator-routing-shadow-eligibility.v1.schema.json and operator-routing-shadow-record.v1.schema.json are Draft 2020-12 fail-closed schemas with hash-bound eligible-case/eligibility identities, bounded features, timestamps, semantic outcome or abstention, primary evidence references and literal no-effect constants.
validated_acceptance.semantic-outcome-separate: The sealed outcome contract accepts only independently reviewed task_correctness or decision_quality labels, or explicit abstention; lifecycle state is not used as semantic correctness.
validated_acceptance.primary-evidence-bound: Every reviewed non-abstaining outcome requires at least one bounded primary evidence reference from github-ci, diff-review, operator-decision, chronik or artifact; abstentions remain unlabeled.
validated_acceptance.proposal-only-no-effect: The schema and implementation hard-bind proposal_only=true and routing/policy/queue/merge/runtime=false; the local capture tool has no MCP/runtime exposure and cannot alter the observed route.
validated_acceptance.privacy-bounded: The record allowlist excludes raw prompts, transcripts, unrestricted argv, private notes and unrestricted manifest payloads; tests assert private manifest content is absent.
validated_acceptance.vibe-lab-consumer: The merged capture contract matches Vibe-Lab experiment 2026-07-23_operator-routing-ml-readiness-shadow Phase B: prospective freeze, authoritative route identity, independent semantic outcome evidence and explicit abstention, while both sides retain no execution authority.
validated_acceptance.tests-and-contracts: Final focused suite passed 23/23; full Grabowski repository validation passed on the converged final head; final-head GitHub CodeQL and validate Python 3.10/3.12 checks passed before merge.

validation: Grabowski PR #410 merged on 2026-07-23 as squash commit 76481e2bf544028d36570776b09495aa0a992248 from reviewed head 133fefbe7c806b86951a9458edb4ec75c6b8d578.
The full reviewed PR diff was 68,287 bytes with SHA-256 7d6317848e6a574f404aa44847effb5ccffbfe53d0a8eabcf087d99b68c8610a; the final self-review was bound to head, base and this diff hash.
Final-head GitHub checks passed: Analyze actions, Analyze python, CodeQL, validate Python 3.10 and validate Python 3.12.
Full local Grabowski make validate on the final converged base passed with durable finalization receipt SHA-256 975ca0cf1ced20cc7c1b38d9d1e17d8b6aeb05409ff18cd664daaa329eb35d4b.
Post-merge readback proved main=76481e2bf544028d36570776b09495aa0a992248 and all five T001 files on main with no content difference from the reviewed PR head.
Vibe-Lab main method.md Phase B remains compatible with this capture contract and explicitly forbids route selection/modification and production authority.
OPERATOR-ML-READINESS-V1-T002 remains planned in lane later and is not started; its independent positive reviewed readiness gate is still unmet.
